message:
fix: weight=non-string accepted on shortest-path / eccentricity / a* (br-r37-c1-blu7u)

nx accepts any hashable (or ``None``) as the ``weight`` argument
on shortest-path, eccentricity, and floyd-warshall functions —
``weight`` is used as an attribute key on edge-data dicts, and
non-string keys / ``None`` silently fall back to the unweighted-
default-of-1.  fnx's PyO3 bindings declare ``weight: str``, so
passing ``weight=5`` (or ``weight=None``, or ``weight=b'w'``,
``weight=(...)``, etc.) raises:

    TypeError: argument 'weight': 'int' object is not an
               instance of 'str'

…BEFORE the algorithm runs, breaking drop-in parity.

Drift was widespread:

  dijkstra_path                 (Rust binding type-rejects)
  dijkstra_path_length          (same)
  bellman_ford_path             (same)
  bellman_ford_path_length      (same)
  astar_path / _length          (same)
  shortest_path / _length       (same)
  single_source_dijkstra/_path  (same)
  multi_source_dijkstra family  (same)
  all_shortest_paths            (same)
  johnson                       (same)
  floyd_warshall family         (same)
  eccentricity                  (separate fast-path; same drift)

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``:

- ``_should_delegate_dijkstra_to_networkx``: route any non-string
  weight to nx.  ``isinstance(weight, str)`` is the only Rust-safe
  branch; everything else (None, ints, floats, bytes, tuples,
  callables) goes through ``_call_networkx_for_parity``.

- ``_should_delegate_bellman_ford_to_networkx`` /
  ``_should_delegate_floyd_warshall_to_networkx`` /
  ``_should_delegate_astar_to_networkx``: same fix at the central
  delegation gate, propagating to every callsite that already uses
  these helpers (~30+ functions).

- ``eccentricity``: has its own fast-path that bypasses the gates;
  add an explicit non-string-weight delegation at the top.

Note: this also fixes a pre-existing latent bug — ``weight=None``
(explicit, not the default ``"weight"``) was already broken on
the Rust path; the gate update incidentally repairs it too.

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_weight_nonstring_parity.py``:
- 7 functions × 4 non-string weight types (int / float / bytes /
  tuple) = 28 parity assertions.
- 3 regressions: ``"weight"`` (string), ``None``, callable lambda.

Full sweep
----------
7076 passed, 0 failed (was 7044 before — +32 new tests, including
the 31 in this file plus 1 elsewhere that gained coverage from
the now-functional ``weight=None`` path).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -55,6 +55,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-bctxc","title":"parity: preferential_attachment returns float counts and reversed pair order vs nx","description":"fnx.preferential_attachment yields (u, v, count) triples with: (1) count as float (2.0) instead of nx's int (2) — preferential attachment is just a degree product so always integer-valued. (2) some pairs in reversed order: fnx returns ('d', 'a', 2.0) where nx returns ('a', 'd', 2). Drop-in code that asserts isinstance(p, int) or that (a, d, 2) in result fails. Fix: delegate to nx for both type and pair-order parity (the underlying degree-product computation is trivial; Rust impl gives no real perf benefit).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:36:16.903798546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:38:39.052813936Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:38:39.050867059Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit c52f0e3. preferential_attachment delegates to nx for int scores and matching pair order. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-bf1wb","title":"parity: has_eulerian_path raises NotImplemented on directed graphs; nx accepts directed","description":"fnx.has_eulerian_path(DiGraph) raises NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for directed type'). nx supports both directed and undirected — a directed graph has an Eulerian path iff at most one vertex has (out_degree - in_degree) = 1, at most one has -1, all others 0, AND the underlying undirected graph is connected. fnx.is_eulerian on directed graphs already works correctly; only has_eulerian_path is over-restricted. Drop-in code that calls has_eulerian_path(digraph) breaks. Fix: delegate to nx for directed inputs (or add native directed support).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:40:11.094534431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:42:05.327792721Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:42:05.325878577Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 8fe2bcb. has_eulerian_path now accepts directed graphs (delegating to nx for the directed case), matching nx's full contract. 10 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-bj91d","title":"parity: random_uniform_k_out_graph + reverse_cuthill_mckee_ordering + flow_matrix_row missing (3 utilities)","description":"Three nx utilities not surfaced by franken_networkx: random_uniform_k_out_graph(n, k, self_loops=True, with_replacement=True, seed=None) from generators.directed (k-out random digraph), reverse_cuthill_mckee_ordering(G, heuristic=None) from utils (bandwidth reduction node ordering), and flow_matrix_row(G, weight=None, dtype=float, solver=lu) from algorithms.centrality.flow_matrix (current-flow centrality kernel). Add Python wrappers via _call_networkx_submodule_for_parity, export at top-level, add to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T21:11:43.252663714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:32:49.845701550Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T21:32:49.843812193Z","close_reason":"Shipped in e3f2e78: random_uniform_k_out_graph + reverse_cuthill_mckee_ordering + flow_matrix_row wrappers. Verified n=8 k=2 seed=42 nodes/edges match, P8 RCM ordering match, K4 flow_matrix_row count match nx.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-blu7u","title":"Parity: weight=int rejected by Rust binding (dijkstra/bellman_ford/astar/floyd-warshall)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:33:21.366080320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T11:34:16.154104884Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-bvf5w","title":"draw_bipartite should accept top_nodes as positional arg matching test expectations","description":"fnx.draw_bipartite has signature (G, **kwargs) where top_nodes must be passed as kwarg. But three tests in test_drawing_extras.py call it positionally as draw_bipartite(graph, top_nodes, ...).

Pre-existing test failures:
- test_draw_bipartite_uses_bipartite_layout_when_pos_missing
- test_draw_bipartite_transformed_graph_uses_local_layout
- test_draw_bipartite_transformed_graph_respects_explicit_pos

The tests reflect the project's intent: fnx's draw_bipartite should accept top_nodes as the second positional argument (a friendly extension of nx's API since nx itself doesn't take top_nodes). Without it, the user has to compute bipartite_sets explicitly which the test explicitly avoids.

Fix: extend signature to (G, top_nodes=None, **kwargs).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:03:41.278034803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:06:29.357628656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:06:29.355658940Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-c4agn","title":"7 more path/traversal fns raise wrong exception on unhashable source","description":"Continuation of br-r37-c1-i9whv. Seven more path-finding/traversal functions raise the wrong exception class when given an unhashable source/target node:

  dijkstra_path             fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  bellman_ford_path         fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  node_connected_component  fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: TypeError
  bfs_edges                 fnx: NetworkXError nx: TypeError
  dfs_edges                 fnx: NetworkXError nx: TypeError
  dfs_tree                  fnx: NetworkXError nx: TypeError
  descendants_at_distance   fnx: NodeNotFound  nx: NetworkXError

Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(TypeError) on these wouldn't trigger. The pattern is the same as the previous beads — fnx falls through to a 'node not in graph' check that silently returns False on unhashable, then raises NodeNotFound or NetworkXError instead of the underlying TypeError.

For descendants_at_distance, nx itself raises NetworkXError (not TypeError, oddly — it pre-validates the source node before dict access). fnx raises NodeNotFound. Match nx's exact class.

Fix: hash-validate at entry of each affected wrapper.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T10:11:20.521819265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T10:19:50.604148102Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T10:19:50.601436797Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-cirha","title":"parity: 34 top-level functions accept positional args that nx made keyword-only","description":"fnx's signatures use POSITIONAL_OR_KEYWORD for params nx declares as KEYWORD_ONLY (after *). Affected: dfs_edges/dfs_tree/dfs_predecessors/dfs_successors/dfs_postorder_nodes/dfs_preorder_nodes (sort_neighbors), random_spanning_tree/random_labeled_tree/random_unlabeled_tree/random_labeled_rooted_tree/random_unlabeled_rooted_tree/random_unlabeled_rooted_forest (seed, multiplicative, number_of_*), connected_watts_strogatz_graph/dense_gnm_random_graph/duplication_divergence_graph/gnm_random_graph/random_lobster_graph/random_powerlaw_tree/generalized_petersen_graph (create_using), directed_edge_swap (nswap, max_tries, seed), from_numpy_array (nodelist), harmonic_diameter (weight), identified_nodes (store_contraction_as), number_of_spanning_trees (weight, root), subgraph_view (filter_node, filter_edge). Forward-compat issue: code using positional form on fnx breaks when ported to nx. Fix: insert * before the documented keyword-only params in each wrapper.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:36:02.353496302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:40:25.924391077Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:40:25.922533471Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 863a001. 25 functions now match nx's POSITIONAL_OR_KEYWORD vs KEYWORD_ONLY param kinds. Code using positional form for these params now correctly raises TypeError on both fnx and nx, preventing forward-compat foot-guns. number_of_spanning_trees got a Python wrapper around the Rust binding. 31 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -3382,6 +3382,15 @@ def _has_negative_edge_weight_for_dijkstra(G, weight):
 def _should_delegate_dijkstra_to_networkx(G, weight):
     if callable(weight):
         return True
+    # br-r37-c1-blu7u: nx accepts any hashable (or None) as ``weight``
+    # (it's used as an attribute key on edge-data dicts; non-string
+    # keys and ``None`` silently fall back to the unweighted-default
+    # of 1).  The Rust binding's PyO3 signature is ``weight: str``,
+    # so int / float / bytes / tuple / None values raise a TypeError
+    # before the algorithm runs.  Delegate any non-string, non-callable
+    # weight (including ``None``) to nx to preserve drop-in parity.
+    if not isinstance(weight, str):
+        return True
     return _has_negative_edge_weight_for_dijkstra(G, weight)
 
 
@@ -3409,15 +3418,28 @@ def _graph_has_nonunit_weight(G, weight):
 
 
 def _should_delegate_bellman_ford_to_networkx(weight):
-    return callable(weight)
+    if callable(weight):
+        return True
+    # br-r37-c1-blu7u: route any non-string weight (including
+    # ``None`` and ints / floats / bytes / tuples) to nx — the
+    # PyO3 binding's ``weight: str`` signature type-rejects.
+    return not isinstance(weight, str)
 
 
 def _should_delegate_floyd_warshall_to_networkx(weight):
-    return callable(weight)
+    if callable(weight):
+        return True
+    # br-r37-c1-blu7u: same — non-string weights would TypeError on
+    # the Rust side; nx silently uses default-1 weights.
+    return not isinstance(weight, str)
 
 
 def _should_delegate_astar_to_networkx(weight, cutoff=None):
-    return callable(weight) or cutoff is not None
+    if callable(weight) or cutoff is not None:
+        return True
+    # br-r37-c1-blu7u: PyO3 binding rejects non-string weights
+    # (including ``None``).
+    return not isinstance(weight, str)
 
 
 def _should_delegate_negative_edge_cycle_to_networkx(G, weight, heuristic):
@@ -5183,6 +5205,15 @@ def eccentricity(G, v=None, sp=None, weight=None):
     if len(G) == 0:
         return {}
 
+    # br-r37-c1-blu7u: nx accepts any hashable as ``weight``; the
+    # Rust ``_raw_eccentricity`` binding has ``weight: str`` and
+    # type-rejects.  Delegate non-string weights to nx (which falls
+    # back to default-1 since no edge has that key).
+    if weight is not None and not isinstance(weight, str) and not callable(weight):
+        return _call_networkx_for_parity("eccentricity", G, v=v, sp=sp, weight=weight)
+    if callable(weight):
+        return _call_networkx_for_parity("eccentricity", G, v=v, sp=sp, weight=weight)
+
     if v is None:
         nodes = list(G)
         single_node = False

@@ -0,0 +1,143 @@
+"""Parity for non-string ``weight`` arguments on shortest-path /
+eccentricity / floyd-warshall / astar functions.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-blu7u.
+
+nx accepts any hashable as ``weight`` — it's used as an attribute
+key on edge-data dicts, and non-string keys silently fall back to
+the unweighted-default-of-1.  fnx's PyO3 bindings declare
+``weight: str``, so passing ``weight=5`` (or any other non-string)
+raises ``TypeError("argument 'weight': 'int' object is not an
+instance of 'str'")`` BEFORE the algorithm runs — breaking
+drop-in parity with nx.
+
+Fix: the central delegation gates (``_should_delegate_dijkstra_to_
+networkx`` etc.) now route non-string, non-callable, non-None
+weight values through nx, where they're handled correctly.
+``eccentricity``, which has its own fast-path, also delegates
+non-string weights to nx.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+NONSTRING_WEIGHTS = [
+    pytest.param(5, id="int"),
+    pytest.param(1.5, id="float"),
+    pytest.param(b"weight", id="bytes"),
+    pytest.param(("w",), id="tuple"),
+]
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Path fns — non-string weights silently fall back to unweighted
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("w", NONSTRING_WEIGHTS)
+def test_dijkstra_path_nonstring_weight_matches_nx(w):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = fnx.dijkstra_path(G, 1, 3, weight=w)
+    n = nx.dijkstra_path(GX, 1, 3, weight=w)
+    assert f == n
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("w", NONSTRING_WEIGHTS)
+def test_dijkstra_path_length_nonstring_weight_matches_nx(w):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = fnx.dijkstra_path_length(G, 1, 3, weight=w)
+    n = nx.dijkstra_path_length(GX, 1, 3, weight=w)
+    assert f == n
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("w", NONSTRING_WEIGHTS)
+def test_bellman_ford_path_nonstring_weight_matches_nx(w):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = fnx.bellman_ford_path(G, 1, 3, weight=w)
+    n = nx.bellman_ford_path(GX, 1, 3, weight=w)
+    assert f == n
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("w", NONSTRING_WEIGHTS)
+def test_astar_path_nonstring_weight_matches_nx(w):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = fnx.astar_path(G, 1, 3, weight=w)
+    n = nx.astar_path(GX, 1, 3, weight=w)
+    assert f == n
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("w", NONSTRING_WEIGHTS)
+def test_shortest_path_nonstring_weight_matches_nx(w):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = fnx.shortest_path(G, 1, 3, weight=w)
+    n = nx.shortest_path(GX, 1, 3, weight=w)
+    assert f == n
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("w", NONSTRING_WEIGHTS)
+def test_eccentricity_nonstring_weight_matches_nx(w):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = fnx.eccentricity(G, weight=w)
+    n = nx.eccentricity(GX, weight=w)
+    assert dict(f) == dict(n)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("w", NONSTRING_WEIGHTS)
+def test_johnson_nonstring_weight_matches_nx(w):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = fnx.johnson(G, weight=w)
+    n = nx.johnson(GX, weight=w)
+    assert {k: dict(v) for k, v in f.items()} == {k: dict(v) for k, v in n.items()}
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Regressions — string weights and None unaffected
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_dijkstra_path_string_weight_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.Graph()
+    G.add_weighted_edges_from([(1, 2, 1.0), (2, 3, 2.0), (1, 3, 5.0)])
+    GX = nx.Graph()
+    GX.add_weighted_edges_from([(1, 2, 1.0), (2, 3, 2.0), (1, 3, 5.0)])
+    assert fnx.dijkstra_path(G, 1, 3, weight="weight") == nx.dijkstra_path(GX, 1, 3, weight="weight")
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_dijkstra_path_none_weight_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    assert fnx.dijkstra_path(G, 1, 3, weight=None) == nx.dijkstra_path(GX, 1, 3, weight=None)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_dijkstra_path_callable_weight_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    f = fnx.dijkstra_path(G, 1, 3, weight=lambda u, v, d: 1.0)
+    n = nx.dijkstra_path(GX, 1, 3, weight=lambda u, v, d: 1.0)
+    assert f == n
